qa_pairs.question: Why do users need to renew their Alliance accounts annually?
qa_pairs.answer: Annual renewal of Alliance user accounts is essential to collect up-to-date information required by granting agencies and to deactivate accounts that are no longer in use.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to renew their Alliance account and when?
qa_pairs.answer: All Alliance users must renew their account between May 6 and June 9, 2026, unless they created their account after January 1 of the current year.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if an Alliance account is not renewed by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Alliance account is not renewed by the deadline, it will be automatically deactivated, preventing access to data, but the data will not be immediately deleted.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any exceptions for recent Alliance account holders regarding the annual renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if an Alliance account was created after January 1, it does not need to be renewed at that time, but the user must still accept new policies and consents on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: Where can an Alliance user find the Privacy Policy and other agreements they have accepted?
qa_pairs.answer: All agreements that an Alliance user has accepted, including Policies, Standards, Terms, and Procedures, can be viewed on the 'Agreements' page within CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: What is considered a primary role within the Alliance account system and why is it important?
qa_pairs.answer: A primary role in the Alliance account system should correspond to a user's main or longest-held position, and it is important for managing multiple roles, facilitating communication, and reporting purposes.

qa_pairs.question: Can a user change their primary role on their Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users with multiple active roles can change their primary role at any time on CCDB's 'Home' page by clicking the 'Make this role primary' checkbox next to the desired role.

qa_pairs.question: What should an Alliance user do if their situation changes, such as moving to a new faculty position or transitioning from a Master's to a PhD program?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Alliance user's situation changes, they must apply for a new role through CCDB's 'My Account' section, and they can choose to disable old roles if they are no longer needed.

qa_pairs.question: If an Alliance account has expired and been deactivated, but the user still needs access to their data, what action should they take?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Alliance account has expired but the user needs access to their data to move it off the Alliance Federation clusters, they should send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca for assistance.

qa_pairs.question: What are the consequences for a faculty member (PI) if they do not renew their Alliance account by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member (PI) does not renew their Alliance account, their account, as well as the accounts of all sponsored users, will be deactivated, pausing jobs and causing their research group to lose file access until the PI's account is reactivated.

qa_pairs.question: How does a Principal Investigator (PI) renew their Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI renews their Alliance account by following a link in an email to the CCDB form, updating contact and research information, completing a user satisfaction survey, updating sponsored roles, and then clicking the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button.

qa_pairs.question: If a Principal Investigator (PI) with a faculty role moves to a different institution, will their sponsored users automatically transfer to their new role?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if a PI with a faculty role moves to a different institution, any user they want to sponsor with their new role must apply for a new role sponsored by the PI's new faculty position, and they may need to be re-added to any Resource Allocation Project (RAP).

qa_pairs.question: What should a Principal Investigator (PI) do to access files of a sponsored user who is no longer under their sponsorship?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI needing to access files of a sponsored user who has moved on should make arrangements with that user for their consent as soon as possible to mitigate the risk of the user becoming incommunicado.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process for a user with a sponsored role (e.g., Doctoral, Masters) to renew their Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: A user with a sponsored role receives an email with a link to the CCDB renewal form, where they must update their contact information, complete a user satisfaction survey, and click 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL,' provided their sponsor has indicated they are continuing.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a sponsored user's Alliance account remain deactivated even after they have completed their own renewal on CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: A sponsored user's Alliance account may remain deactivated even after their own renewal because their sponsor has not yet renewed or reactivated their own account and confirmed the sponsored user's role.

qa_pairs.question: What should a sponsored user do if they mistakenly renewed their Alliance account with the wrong role?
qa_pairs.answer: If a sponsored user mistakenly renewed their account with the wrong role, they should create a new role with their current information by logging into CCDB and going to 'My Account' then 'Apply for a new role', and their sponsor will then confirm this new role.

qa_pairs.question: Who should an Alliance user contact if their account renewal question is not answered in the FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Alliance user's account renewal question is not answered in the FAQ, they should send an email to renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca for assistance.
